orientation.description: Canvas orientation. 'vertical' → 1080×1920 (9:16, default), 'horizontal' → 1920×1080 (16:9). Resolves width/height and picks each frame's layout.

capHighlight.description: Karaoke spoken-word highlight color (hex). Defaults to #FFD24A; independent of each frame's accent so captions read as one system.

description.description: YouTube description (2-4 sentences). Written to meta.json; not used at render.

hashtags.description: YouTube hashtags (3-6, lowercase, no #). Written to meta.json; not used at render.

tags.description: YouTube search-keyword tags (5-10 phrases). Written to meta.json; not used at render.

audio.description: Single full-script narration path relative to videos/<slug>/. Written by the alignment step; replaces the old per-scene scenes[].audio.

voiceStyle.description: Metadata for LLM pipeline; not used at render

id.description: Unique kebab-case id → filename and composition-id

type.description: Frame type selector. Every type renders in both orientations at the canvas size resolved from config.orientation (vertical 1080×1920 by default, horizontal 1920×1080).

typeUsage.cover: DEFAULT for the opening scene / topic intro. A safe general choice. Include an 'imagePrompt' for a topic-relevant HOOK photograph rendered full-bleed behind the headline.
typeUsage.stats: use when the scene centers on ONE striking number or figure (e.g. an audit-finding rate, a calibration interval, an uncertainty percentage).
typeUsage.diagram: DEFAULT for general explanatory content — a set of labelled ideas/steps with no dedicated visual (e.g. the stages of a process). A safe general choice when nothing more specific fits.
typeUsage.cta: DEFAULT for the closing scene / takeaway / call to action. Include an 'imagePrompt' for a CONCLUSION photograph rendered full-bleed behind the headline.
typeUsage.quote: use ONLY when the narration is a pull quote, principle, or citation. Show the quote text and attribution.
typeUsage.equipment-register: use ONLY when the narration is about laboratory equipment and its calibration status (Clause 6.4). Shows an equipment register: a grid of instruments each with a calibration-status chip (calibrated / due / overdue) and a due date.
typeUsage.calibration-cert: use ONLY when the narration is about a calibration certificate, metrological traceability, or measurement uncertainty for a specific instrument (Clause 6.5). Shows a certificate card with instrument, dates, uncertainty and the standard it is traceable to.
typeUsage.document-control: use ONLY when the narration is about the documentation hierarchy or document/record control (Clause 8.3/7.5). Shows a tiered pyramid from the quality manual down to records.
typeUsage.audit-trail: use ONLY when the narration walks through a corrective-action / CAPA path (Clause 8.7): a finding, its root cause, the corrective action, and its verification. Shows the four stages as a connected flow.
typeUsage.competence-matrix: use ONLY when the narration is about personnel competence and authorization (Clause 6.2). Shows a matrix of staff (rows) versus methods/tasks (columns) with authorized / training / not-authorized marks.
typeUsage.traceability-chain: use ONLY when the narration describes metrological traceability as a chain from the SI unit down to the measurement. Shows the linked nodes (SI → national standard → reference → working standard → measurement).
typeUsage.nonconformance: use ONLY when the narration is about a specific nonconformity / nonconforming work and its severity (Clause 7.10/8.7). Shows a severity banner (CRITICAL/MAJOR/MINOR), the clause reference, the finding, and the corrective action.
typeUsage.reagent-prep: use ONLY when the narration walks through the MATH of preparing a reagent or solution — dilution (C1V1 = C2V2), molarity, mass from concentration, %(w/v), or ppm. Shows a formula card with the equation(s) and a variable legend.
typeUsage.statistics: use ONLY when the narration is about the STATISTICAL formulas used to evaluate test-result reliability or QC (Clause 7.7/6.5) — mean, sample standard deviation (SD), coefficient of variation (CV%), or similar. Shows a formula card with the equation(s) and a variable legend.
typeUsage.uncertainty: use ONLY when the narration derives a MEASUREMENT UNCERTAINTY formula (Clause 6.5/7.6) — standard uncertainty of the mean, combined standard uncertainty, or expanded uncertainty (U = k·u_c). Shows a formula card with the equation(s) and a variable legend.
typeUsage.photo: use when the scene is best shown with a REALISTIC PHOTOGRAPH filling the screen (a real lab, an instrument, a technician at work) rather than a diagram. Write a vivid 'imagePrompt'; the system generates the picture, which is ANIMATED (add an optional 'anim' to pick the camera move — slow zoom, pan, focus-pull, breathe — matching the scene's mood). Do NOT overuse — prefer concept-specific types when the content is schematic.
typeUsage.photo-split: like 'photo' but pairs the generated (animated) photograph with a short block of text (headline + body) beside it — use when you want a realistic image AND a sentence of explanation on screen together. Optional 'anim' picks the picture's motion.

anim.description: Optional motion spec for any IMAGE frame (photo, photo-split, cover, cta) — how the generated picture moves. Omit to accept a gentle ken-burns-in push. Choose values that fit the scene's mood.

preset.description: Camera move across the still. ken-burns-in/out = slow zoom in/out; pan-* = drift across the frame (good for establishing/wide lab shots); push-diagonal = zoom + diagonal drift; focus-pull = starts blurred then snaps sharp (a reveal); breathe = very subtle living pulse.

focus.description: Where the move anchors (transform-origin / point of interest). Default 'center'.

overlay.description: Optional atmosphere layer on top of the image: sweep = a light glint crossing once; vignette = breathing dark edges; grain = subtle film grain; glow = accent edge glow. Default 'none'.

start.description: Computed by scripts/align-captions.mjs from real narration timestamps once that pipeline has run; hand-authored otherwise.

duration.description: Computed by scripts/align-captions.mjs from real narration timestamps once that pipeline has run; hand-authored otherwise.

captions.description: Naturally-split caption chunks displayed sequentially during the scene. Also doubles as the scene's expected narration text (chunks joined with spaces) for scripts/align-captions.mjs.

captionTiming.description: Populate-computed, not hand-authored. Written by the alignment step from real Whisper word timestamps: one {text,start,end,words} entry per captions[] chunk, start/end in scene-local seconds. words[] carries per-token {text,start,end} for the karaoke highlight. If absent, populate.js falls back to evenly spacing captions[] across duration and synthesizes words[].

words.description: Per-word timing for the karaoke highlight; one entry per whitespace token, scene-local seconds.

items.description: Sticker chip labels (diagram). Each entry is either a plain string label, or { label, color } for a per-chip background color — do not mix in unsupported shapes like nested objects.

statLabel.description: Short label under the stat, e.g. 'of findings are documentation gaps' (stats)

subheadline.description: Secondary line under the headline, one short sentence (cta)

title.description: Short heading label for the frame's hero content — meaning differs by type: for equipment-register a register heading, e.g. 'Equipment Register'; for document-control a heading, e.g. 'Controlled Documents'; for competence-matrix a heading, e.g. 'Competence Matrix'; for traceability-chain a heading, e.g. 'Traceability to the SI'; for reagent-prep/statistics/uncertainty an optional card heading, e.g. 'Dilution Formula' or 'QC Statistics'. (equipment-register, document-control, competence-matrix, traceability-chain, reagent-prep, statistics, uncertainty)

formulas.description: 1-3 formulas shown as a stacked equation card (reagent-prep, statistics, uncertainty). Each entry is { name, latex }: 'name' is a short plain-text label above the equation (e.g. 'Mean', 'Dilution'); 'latex' is a KaTeX math string rendered in display mode (commands like frac, sqrt, sum, bar — see the GOLDEN EXAMPLES below for the exact JSON escaping to use for LaTeX backslashes).

name.description: Short plain-text label above the equation, e.g. 'Standard Deviation (SD)'.

latex.description: KaTeX math string for the equation, rendered in display mode.

legend.description: Optional 'where:' variable key shown under the formula(s) (reagent-prep, statistics, uncertainty). Each entry is { sym, desc }: 'sym' is a short KaTeX snippet for the variable (e.g. an x with a subscript i, or an x with a bar over it), 'desc' is its plain-text meaning (e.g. 'individual measured values').

sym.description: Short KaTeX snippet for the variable, rendered inline.

parts.description: Instruments in the equipment register (equipment-register). 1–6 entries, each { label, status, due? }.

status.description: Calibration status. 'calibrated' = in date (green), 'due' = due soon (amber), 'overdue' = past due (red).

due.description: Short due-date label, e.g. 'Due Mar 2027' or 'Overdue 12 days'. Optional.

instrument.description: Instrument name / serial being calibrated, e.g. 'Reference Thermometer, S/N 4471' (calibration-cert)

calDate.description: Calibration date as DISPLAY text, e.g. '14 Feb 2026' (calibration-cert)

dueDate.description: Recalibration due date as DISPLAY text, e.g. '14 Feb 2027' (calibration-cert)

uncertainty.description: Expanded measurement uncertainty as DISPLAY text, e.g. '± 0.02 °C (k = 2)' (calibration-cert)

traceableTo.description: What the calibration is traceable to, e.g. 'National standard (NMI) / SI' (calibration-cert)

tiers.description: Document-hierarchy tiers, top (broadest) to bottom (most specific) (document-control). 2–5 entries, each { label, sub? }.

finding.description: For audit-trail: the nonconformity/observation found. For nonconformance: the nonconformity statement. Keep it one short sentence. (audit-trail, nonconformance)

rootCause.description: Identified root cause of the finding (audit-trail)

columns.description: Method/task column headers for the competence matrix, e.g. ['Titration','GC-MS','Reporting']. 2–4 entries. (competence-matrix)

rows.description: Staff rows for the competence matrix (competence-matrix). 1–5 entries, each { name, marks }.

marks.description: One mark per column, same order as columns[]. 'yes' = authorized, 'no' = not authorized, 'training' = in training.

nodes.description: Metrological traceability chain nodes, from the SI unit down to the measurement (traceability-chain). 3–5 entries, each { label, sub? }.

sub.description: Optional short descriptor, e.g. 'NMI' or 'the lab's best'.

clause.description: ISO/IEC 17025 clause reference the nonconformity is raised against, e.g. 'Clause 6.4.1'. Optional. (nonconformance)

correctiveAction.description: One short corrective-action statement, e.g. 'Quarantine results; recalibrate before reuse.' (nonconformance)

imagePrompt.description: REQUIRED for photo / photo-split / cover / cta. A vivid, specific description of the photograph to generate for this scene (subject, setting, lighting, style) — the system generates the image from it. Describe a realistic photo; do NOT include text/words in the image. For cover it is the HOOK image and for cta the CONCLUSION image, rendered full-bleed behind the headline. (photo, photo-split, cover, cta)

image.description: System-generated — DO NOT author this. Filled by the image-generation step with the rendered picture (a data URI); defaults to a placeholder. (photo, photo-split, cover, cta)

body.description: One short supporting sentence shown beside the image. Optional. (photo-split)
